Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for StormCloud correspondence using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or similar). Find Jae-won Kim's email to support@moveops.com asking about his lease documents, Omar Ibrahim's reply promising to check with the relocation team, and the broader StormCloud account email history including the original contract negotiation with Tom Wexler and Mina's flight-rebooking resolution email documenting the $500 goodwill credit and $320 rebooking fee coverage.
OE 2: Search emails for Liam Park's escalation using search_emails (query: "Liam Park" or similar). Find the formal complaint from liam.park@stormcloud.io to Mina listing all open failures: Jae-won's unsigned lease with expiring temporary housing, the goodwill credit that does not appear on the invoice, Simone Richter's desk reimbursement stuck with no timeline, and the unanswered Ridgeline Robotics referral from Anya Petrova. This is the CFO-level escalation referenced in the prompt.
OE 3: Search emails for the lease background using search_emails (query: "UrbanNest" or "guarantor" or "Barton Creek" or "Carmen Reyes" or similar). Find Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest's email flagging that Jae-won's Austin lease at Barton Creek Flats is still pending the corporate guarantor packet, the landlord will not countersign without it. Find Suki Patel's reply promising to send the packet by end of week. No evidence of actual follow-through after Suki's reply.
OE 4: Search emails for Simone Richter's reimbursement using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "standing desk" or "reimbursement" or similar). Find Simone Richter's email (simone.richter@stormcloud.io) to Mina about her $340 standing desk claim submitted March 25 still showing Pending Review. Find Mina's forward to Marcus Thorne asking to release it. Find Marcus's response refusing all exceptions, all pending stipend reimbursements frozen until the fraud review is complete regardless of client, employee, or amount.
OE 5: Search emails for the Ridgeline Robotics referral using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "Anya Petrova" or "Kenji Watanabe" or similar). Find Anya Petrova's (anya.petrova@stormcloud.io) warm referral email to David Chen CC'ing Mina and Kenji Watanabe (kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com), HR Director at Ridgeline Robotics, for an Austin relocation batch of 5 employees. Find Kenji's follow-up email asking to connect. Find David Chen's belated reply on April 24 and Mina Hashimoto's recovery outreach email to Kenji.
OE 6: Search Slack for StormCloud internal discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or similar) and retrieve thread context using conversations_replies where needed. Key findings span multiple channels: Omar's post in #customer-support (C007) tagging Jae-won's lease request as low priority because Airtable showed Completed and apartment Confirmed, Mina's full reconstruction message in #executive (C004) detailing the chain of failures and listing required next steps, David Chen's celebration of the Ridgeline referral in #customer-engagement (C002) with Mina's reply calling it a trust signal but no follow-up action taken, Marcus Thorne's discovery in #finance (C005) that the $500 goodwill credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 not the live StormCloud invoice INV-2026-0319, and Mina's request to Marcus in #finance about releasing Simone's claim with no resolution.
OE 7: Search Slack for the ops audit of Jae-won's lease path using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Jae-won" or "lease" or "guarantor" or similar) focusing on #operations (C006). Find Chloe Vance's audit confirming: Jae-won is still in temporary corporate housing (not a finalized lease), the guarantor packet was never sent to UrbanNest or the landlord, the completion status in Airtable propagated bad data into support which is why Omar treated the request as low priority, and the Linear ticket should have been reopened when the landlord packet was outstanding.
OE 8: Search CRM for the StormCloud account using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud"). Find company_stormcloud (StormCloud Analytics, Denver CO, 85 employees, SaaS/Analytics, Customer type). Search for deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "StormCloud" or similar). Find deal_stormcloud_won ($26,400 for 3 relocations at $8,800 each, closedWon, January 2026). Check engagement history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]).
OE 9: Search CRM for the Ridgeline Robotics referral using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") and crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe" or similar). Find Ridgeline Robotics company record (crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2, Prospect, 180 employees, Austin TX, Robotics). Find CRM lead for Kenji Watanabe (kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com, HR Director, Hot rating, referred by Anya Petrova). Find CRM deal "Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)" at Qualified To Buy stage ($8,750, close date May 8).
OE 10: Search Airtable for StormCloud relocation records by first listing bases using airtable_list_bases and tables using airtable_list_tables, then using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Company", value: "StormCloud Analytics") on the Relocations table. Find 4 relocation records: Jamie Reeves (recReloc00000017, Completed, Denver→Austin, includes incident note about flight booked on wrong date with $500 goodwill credit), Sasha Petrov (recReloc00000018, Completed, Denver→Austin), Colin Nakamura (recReloc00000019, Completed, Denver→Seattle), and Jae-won Kim (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, Status: Completed, Seattle→Austin, note: "Lease pending — UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on corporate guarantor packet. Temporary housing confirmed, permanent placement with Barton Creek Flats still awaiting countersignature."). Also find the Client Accounts record (recAcct000000063) showing Mina Hashimoto as Account Manager, 3 total relocations, Active status.
OE 11: Search Linear for StormCloud-related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or similar). Find three tickets: (1) the original Jae-won relocation ticket (linear_issue_c97359216490, "StormCloud Relocation - Jae-won Kim - SEA → AUS", closed, assigned to Suki Patel, description mentions UrbanNest guarantor request), (2) Omar's low-priority follow-up ticket (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, #412, "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents", priority 4/low, unassigned), and (3) Mina's P1 incident reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, "StormCloud account incident reconstruction, Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop", assigned to Mina Hashimoto). Use linear_get_issue to read full descriptions.
OE 12: Search QuickBooks for StormCloud billing using quickbooks_search_customers (criteria for "StormCloud" or similar) and quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_read_invoice. Find StormCloud Analytics invoice inv_stormcloud_01 ($26,400 for 3 premium relocations, 2 Denver→Austin at $8,800 each and 1 Denver→Seattle at $8,800). The $500 goodwill credit misapplication is primarily confirmed via Marcus's Slack finding that the credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live invoice INV-2026-0319.
OE 13: Look up contact details for key people using contacts_search_contacts. Look up Jae-won Kim (query: "Jae-won") to find jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io. Look up Julian Brooks (query: "Julian Brooks") to find julian.brooks@moveops.com. Look up Mina Hashimoto (query: "Mina Hashimoto") to find mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. Look up Omar Ibrahim (query: "Omar Ibrahim") to find omar.ibrahim@moveops.com. These are needed for the write actions that follow.
OE 14: Update Jae-won Kim's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: "recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin", fields: {...}}]). Update the record to accurately reflect that the lease is unsigned, Jae-won is still in temporary corporate housing, and the corporate guarantor packet was never sent to the landlord at Barton Creek Flats. Correct any status or notes fields that incorrectly indicate the relocation is completed.
OE 15: Create a CRM engagement note on the StormCloud account using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: summary of all open issues, company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]). Content should document: (1) Jae-won Kim's Austin lease unsigned, still in temp housing, guarantor packet never sent, (2) $500 goodwill credit from flight rebooking posted to wrong estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of live invoice INV-2026-0319, (3) Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement frozen under blanket stipend hold with no individual exception, (4) Ridgeline Robotics referral from Anya Petrova, delayed response, David and Mina sent recovery outreach on April 24-25.
OE 16: Send email to Jae-won Kim using send_email (sender: omar.ibrahim@moveops.com, recipients: ["jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io"]). Content should honestly acknowledge that his Austin lease at Barton Creek Flats was not finalized due to outstanding corporate guarantor paperwork, apologize for the delay and for the earlier response that treated it as a routine document request, confirm the issue has been escalated internally, and provide a commitment to follow up with a resolution timeline.
OE 17: Create a 30-minute calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "StormCloud Account Recovery" or similar, start_datetime and end_datetime for a 30-minute slot this week, attendees: ["julian.brooks@moveops.com", "mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", "omar.ibrahim@moveops.com"], description: covering the recovery plan walkthrough for all open StormCloud issues).
OE 18: Create Linear tracking tickets for unresolved items that do not already have dedicated tickets, using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations" or similar). The existing tickets cover the overall incident reconstruction (linear_issue_a49b4d199139) and Jae-won's lease follow-up (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0), so new tickets should target distinct untracked items. Candidates include Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement frozen under the blanket stipend hold and needing an individual review or exception path, and the StormCloud goodwill credit correction where the $500 credit must be moved from cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 to live invoice INV-2026-0319. The Ridgeline Robotics follow-up already has a CRM deal tracking it but may also warrant a ticket if no outbound action is being tracked in Linear. Individual tickets should reference the incident reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139) as context.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

1 An Airtable record update was executed (via airtable_update_records) targeting Jae-won Kim's relocation record (record ID recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) in the Relocations table (tblRelocations01) of the MoveOps Operations base (appMoveOpsOps001).

outcome

✓Run #4The final response explains 'She then batch-closed the Linear ticket and left Airtable as "Completed." When you saw Jae-won's 4/13 email, the systems told you everything was done — that's why you parked it. The records were wrong, not your judgment call in isolation.'
